iterate_latest.summary: Consumer side of Option B: campaign-store reads a producer-owned top-level lifecycle status (status.json wins / campaign.md frontmatter fallback) via the new campaign-status-json.ts split; selectActiveCampaigns shows iff active, hides draft/complete, legacy no-status falls back to done<total. server tsc + 1430/1430 vitest; client tsc + lint + 1420/1420 vitest; F0.5 web E2E tests_run=1 (active/draft/complete/legacy fixture).

surface_verification.note: Isolated built stack seeded with 4 campaigns: active(status:active,0/2) + legacy(no-status,1/2) SHOWN; draft(status:draft,0/2) + complete(status:complete,2/2) HIDDEN. Live /api/campaigns also curl-verified.

degraded.condition: server `npm run lint` (oxlint) binary absent in junctioned node_modules (pre-existing worktree-hydration gap). New/changed server files linted clean via the client's pinned oxlint binary; client `npm run lint` passed.

behaviors.behavior: Server reads top-level lifecycle status from status.json into Campaign.status
behaviors.evidence: campaign-store.test.ts 'reads the top-level lifecycle status from status.json' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Server reads lifecycle status from campaign.md frontmatter when no status.json status
status.json top-level status wins over frontmatter
Invalid / absent status resolves to null (legacy)
Client selectActiveCampaigns: active shown, draft+complete hidden, legacy done<total fallback
Live board shows ONLY active + legacy campaigns (draft/complete hidden)
